parts.text:
You are executing one step of Prodia's LLM pipeline.
Follow the provided step prompt exactly.
Return strict JSON only. Do not include Markdown or commentary.

{
  "stepPrompt": "You are the Prodia builder agent.\n\nImplement the RequirementSpec as a local inspectable artifact. Return structured JSON with a BuildPlan and file drafts.\n\nSubmission context:\n- The artifact may be used as one of Prodia's public hackathon submission examples.\n- Build the smallest product experience that proves the concept on the first screen.\n- The artifact should feel like a usable mini-product, not a slide, essay, generic dashboard, or static mockup.\n- Static sample data is preferred when it keeps the artifact reliable and inspectable.\n\nTemplate pattern boundary:\n- Implement the concept through the selected `templatePatternId` when provided. If missing, infer the closest safe pattern from the RequirementSpec.\n- Supported safe patterns:\n  - `source_to_mission`: source selector, level/mode selector, route list, selected step detail, completion clue.\n  - `evidence_decision_board`: candidate cards, evidence/risk controls, priority lanes, next action output.\n  - `signal_map`: zone map, evidence layer selector, selected zone detail, exploration path.\n  - `transformation_studio`: input panel, transformation lens controls, before/after output, difference rationale.\n  - `boundary_simulator`: scenario controls, risk/usefulness meter, human approval point, safe next step.\n  - `guided_explainer_path`: persona/question tabs, adaptive explanation, example, first action.\n  - `remix_roulette`: draw/lock/remix controls, source cards, generated next experiment, rationale.\n  - `ops_steward_console`: finding filters, evidence cards, human action queue, system verification status.\n- The pattern must change the UI and state behavior, not only the title or copy.\n- Do not collapse every pattern into a generic dashboard, meeting room, or static card grid.\n\nRules:\n- No external API calls.\n- No secrets.\n- No paid services.\n- Use static sample data unless explicitly approved.\n- The core interaction must be visible in the UI.\n- The first screen must make the product concept clear.\n- Preserve human / agent / system distinctions where relevant.\n- Show what AI transformed: source signal, input material, repo, trend, question, or user context.\n- Include at least one user-controlled state change such as select, filter, score, compare, simulate, route, reveal, or move.\n- Make the output area concrete: mission, plan, scorecard, map, transformed artifact, decision memo, explanation path, or next action.\n- Declare the Prodia MVP Contract in `mvpContract`: firstScreenValue, coreInteraction, stateChange, inspectableOutput, staticDataBoundary, requiredFiles, nonGoals, and forbiddenDependencies.\n- Keep source inspectability high. File names, data shape, and generated output should be easy to explain.\n- Avoid vague panels named only \"Insights\", \"Dashboard\", \"AI Output\", or \"Summary\" unless the content is specific and actionable.\n- Avoid a three-column meeting-room layout unless the RequirementSpec explicitly demands it and explains why it is novel.\n- Do not invent unsupported claims about real external products. If live facts are not available, label data as sample.\n\nRequired file coverage:\n- `README.md`: explain the product, user moment, interaction, and limits.\n- `metadata.json`: include title, oneLiner, targetUser, userMoment, coreInteraction, process, architecture, sourcePlan, and risks.\n- `manifest.json`: list all files and identify the entrypoint.\n- `source/app/page.tsx`: entrypoint.\n- `source/components/ProductWorkspace.tsx`: main interactive UI.\n- `source/data/product.ts`: static data.\n- `source/styles.css`: artifact-specific styling.\n- `validation/self-review.json`: score the artifact against Prodia's MVP criteria.\n\nDesign bar:\n- The first viewport should contain the product name, input/control area, transformation or reasoning surface, and output/result area.\n- Text must be concise enough to fit inside cards and buttons.\n- Controls should be obvious: buttons, tabs, segmented controls, sliders, filters, or selectable cards.\n- Empty states should not appear in the representative demo.\n- The artifact must still be understandable if embedded in Prodia's Project Demo page.\n\nSelf-review before returning:\n- Does the user have something real to click or choose?\n- Does the output change after interaction?\n- Is the product different from a generic AI dashboard or summarizer?\n- Can a judge explain what AI did after 10 seconds?\n- Are all claims safe with static sample data?\n\nExpected JSON shape:\n{\n  \"requirementSpecId\": \"string\",\n  \"framework\": \"next_static_artifact\",\n  \"files\": [\n    {\n      \"path\": \"source/app/page.tsx\",\n      \"purpose\": \"string\",\n      \"content\": \"string\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"implementationNotes\": [\"string\"],\n  \"knownRisks\": [\"string\"],\n  \"submissionReadiness\": {\n    \"firstScreenValue\": \"string\",\n    \"coreInteraction\": \"string\",\n    \"stateChange\": \"string\",\n    \"inspectableOutput\": \"string\",\n    \"staticDataBoundary\": \"string\",\n    \"remainingWeakness\": \"string\"\n  },\n  \"mvpContract\": {\n    \"firstScreenValue\": \"string\",\n    \"coreInteraction\": \"string\",\n    \"stateChange\": \"string\",\n    \"inspectableOutput\": \"string\",\n    \"staticDataBoundary\": \"string\",\n    \"requiredFiles\": [\n      \"README.md\",\n      \"metadata.json\",\n      \"manifest.json\",\n      \"source/app/page.tsx\",\n      \"source/components/ProductWorkspace.tsx\",\n      \"source/data/product.ts\",\n      \"validation/self-review.json\"\n    ],\n    \"nonGoals\": [\n      \"No live external API integration\",\n      \"No login-only experience\",\n      \"No paid API dependency\",\n      \"No external publishing\"\n    ],\n    \"forbiddenDependencies\": [\n      \"external API\",\n      \"secret\",\n      \"login-only flow\",\n      \"paid API\",\n      \"external publishing\"\n    ]\n  }\n}\n",
  "structuredInput": {
    "version": 1,
    "runId": "findy_gemini_evidence",

    "instructions": {
      "returnFormat": "strict_json_only",
      "noExternalPublish": true,
      "noPaidApiRequired": true,
      "noSecrets": true
    },
    "previousResponseStatus": {
      "requiredPreviousSteps": [
        "concept",
        "agent-router",
        "requirements"
      ],
      "missingRequiredPreviousResponses": [],
      "missingPreviousResponses": [],
      "note": "Required upstream response.json files are present for this step."
    },
    "concept": {
      "candidates": [
        {
          "id": "concept_otayori_route",
          "title": "Otayori Route",
          "oneLiner": "Turn a long school or civic notice into family action cards with source evidence and uncertainty.",
          "sourceSignalIds": [
            "signal_school_notice_route"
          ],
          "notObviousInsight": "The value is not a shorter summary; it is making actors, dates, materials, and open questions visible enough for a household to act.",
          "researchAngleUsed": "domain_pain_to_tool",
          "combinationUsed": "remix_school_notice_action_route",
          "remixUsed": "remix_school_notice_action_route",
          "sourceProductUsed": "source_crossbeam_pattern",
          "originalCoreTransferred": "Dense document to guided action workflow.",
          "newThemeApplied": "school notice family action routing",
          "ideaMoveUsed": "output_form_shift",
          "topicCardUsed": "topic_school_parent_communication",
          "evidenceUsed": [
            "research_school_notice_route",
            "signal_school_notice_route"
          ],
          "noveltyTypeUsed": "new_workflow",
          "surfaceAudience": "family/local citizen",
          "aiAppreciationAudience": "AI-curious professional",
          "surfaceTheme": "education",
          "surfacePattern": "daily_utility",
          "aiMechanismPattern": "workflow_generation",
          "templatePatternId": "source_to_mission",
          "templatePatternReason": "The first screen can show original source, extracted actions, and provenance together.",
          "whyNormalUserCares": "They can see what to do today and what still needs confirmation.",
          "whyAIUserCares": "The artifact exposes extraction, routing, uncertainty, and provenance instead of hiding them behind a summary.",
          "aiMechanism": "Extract actors, dates, materials, and questions, then route them into action cards with source references.",
          "targetUser": "Parent or local resident handling a long notice.",
          "userMoment": "After receiving a long notice and before deciding who needs to do what.",
          "coreInteraction": "Paste or inspect a notice, then review action cards, confirmation questions, and source evidence.",
          "whyDifferentFromRecentArtifacts": "It targets a normal household workflow rather than another AI-operator dashboard.",
          "artifactShape": "workspace",
          "antiGenericChecks": [
            "Not just summary",
            "Source references remain visible",
            "Uncertainty remains visible"
          ],
          "sharpnessChecks": [
            "Action cards are visible in the first screen",
            "At least one source reference is attached to each card"
          ],
          "risks": [
            "Generated guidance may be mistaken for official instruction"
          ],
          "successCriteria": [
            "A user can identify one next action",
            "A user can see what remains uncertain",
            "A user can trace a card to source text"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "selectedConcept": {
        "id": "concept_otayori_route",
        "title": "Otayori Route",
        "oneLiner": "Turn a long school or civic notice into family action cards with source evidence and uncertainty.",

        ],
        "notObviousInsight": "The value is not a shorter summary; it is making actors, dates, materials, and open questions visible enough for a household to act.",

        ],

        "aiAppreciationAudience": "AI-curious professional",
        "surfaceTheme": "education",
        "surfacePattern": "daily_utility",
        "aiMechanismPattern": "workflow_generation",
        "templatePatternId": "source_to_mission",
        "templatePatternReason": "The first screen can show original source, extracted actions, and provenance together.",
        "whyNormalUserCares": "They can see what to do today and what still needs confirmation.",
        "whyAIUserCares": "The artifact exposes extraction, routing, uncertainty, and provenance instead of hiding them behind a summary.",
        "aiMechanism": "Extract actors, dates, materials, and questions, then route them into action cards with source references.",
        "targetUser": "Parent or local resident handling a long notice.",
        "userMoment": "After receiving a long notice and before deciding who needs to do what.",
        "coreInteraction": "Paste or inspect a notice, then review action cards, confirmation questions, and source evidence.",
        "whyDifferentFromRecentArtifacts": "It targets a normal household workflow rather than another AI-operator dashboard.",
        "artifactShape": "workspace",
        "antiGenericChecks": [
          "Not just summary",
          "Source references remain visible",
          "Uncertainty remains visible"
        ],
        "sharpnessChecks": [
          "Action cards are visible in the first screen",
          "At least one source reference is attached to each card"

        ],
        "successCriteria": [

          "A user can see what remains uncertain",
          "A user can trace a card to source text"
        ]
      },
      "conceptRunSummary": {
        "id": "findy_local_concept_chain_check",
        "generatedAt": "2026-06-27T00:00:00.000Z",
        "inputCombinationRunId": "findy_local_combination_chain_check",
        "selectedConceptId": "concept_otayori_route",
        "whySelected": "It is a small, explainable artifact with clear research, combination, and provenance links.",
        "risksForBuilder": [
          "Keep source references and uncertainty visible."
        ]
      }
    },
    "assignment": {
      "selectedAgentIds": [
        "agent_a",
        "agent_c"
      ],
      "reason": "Smoke response for checking that creator agent assignment flows into later pipeline steps.",
      "assignmentNotes": [
        "Triage should keep the concept operationally concrete.",
        "Explainer should make the resulting artifact understandable."
      ]
    },
    "requirementSpec": {
      "id": "req_otayori_mvp_v1",
      "conceptId": "concept_otayori_route",
      "ownerAgentId": "agent_a",
      "mvpGoal": "Allow a user to paste a school or civic notice and immediately see extracted action cards, each clearly linked to its source and indicating any uncertainty, all within a single, inspectable screen.",
      "screens": [
        {
          "name": "Notice to Action Workspace",
          "purpose": "To enable users to transform a dense notice into actionable cards with transparent provenance and uncertainty, without leaving the page.",
          "components": [
            "Notice Input Area (text field for pasting notice)",
            "Original Notice Display (read-only text area showing the full notice, with highlightable sections)",
            "Action Cards Display (container for generated action cards)",
            "Action Card (component showing actor, action description, due date, materials, confirmation question, uncertainty indicator, and source reference link)",
            "Source Reference Link (interactive element within an Action Card to highlight text in Original Notice Display)",
            "Uncertainty Indicator (visual cue on Action Card for uncertain information)",
            "Disclaimer (prominent text regarding AI generation and need for verification)"
          ],
          "interactions": [
            "User pastes or types notice into 'Notice Input Area', triggering action card generation.",
            "User reviews generated 'Action Cards' in the 'Action Cards Display'.",
            "User clicks on a 'Source Reference Link' within an 'Action Card' to highlight the corresponding text in the 'Original Notice Display'."
          ]
        }
      ],
      "dataModel": [
        {
          "name": "Notice",
          "fields": [
            "id: string",
            "rawText: string",
            "ingestionTimestamp: datetime"
          ]
        },
        {
          "name": "ActionCard",
          "fields": [
            "id: string",
            "noticeId: string (references Notice)",
            "actor: string (e.g., 'Parent', 'Student')",
            "actionDescription: string",
            "dueDate: date (optional)",
            "materials: string[] (optional list of items needed)",
            "sourceReferenceIds: string[] (references SourceReference)",
            "uncertaintyFlag: boolean",
            "confirmationQuestion: string (if uncertaintyFlag is true)"
          ]
        },
        {
          "name": "SourceReference",
          "fields": [
            "id: string",
            "noticeId: string (references Notice)",
            "startIndex: number (character index in rawText)",
            "endIndex: number (character index in rawText)",
            "textSnippet: string (the actual text segment)"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "acceptanceCriteria": [
        "A user can paste a long notice into the input area.",
        "Upon processing, the 'Action Cards Display' populates with at least one action card.",
        "A user can identify one next action from the displayed action cards.",
        "A user can see what information remains uncertain via a dedicated indicator on relevant action cards.",
        "A user can trace any piece of information on an action card directly to its source text by clicking a reference link, which highlights the relevant segment in the 'Original Notice Display'.",
        "Action cards are prominently visible on the first screen immediately after processing.",
        "At least one source reference is visibly attached to each generated action card."
      ],
      "nonGoals": [
        "Developing a full-fledged platform for notice management (e.g., saving, editing, organizing multiple notices).",
        "Allowing users to edit or modify the generated action cards.",
        "Implementing user authentication or personalized profiles.",
        "Integrating with external calendars, task managers, or communication tools.",
        "Providing multi-language support (initially English only).",
        "Supporting file uploads (initially text paste only)."
      ],
      "safetyConstraints": [
        "A clear and prominent disclaimer must be displayed, stating that the generated guidance is AI-produced and not official instruction, requiring user verification.",
        "The original source text of the notice must always be visible and easily accessible for users to cross-reference and verify generated actions.",
        "Uncertainties or ambiguities in the extracted information must be explicitly highlighted on the action cards and accompanied by confirmation questions to prompt user awareness and verification."
      ]
    },
    "missingPreviousResponses": []
  }
}
